---
title: "LRPHS04: Regularized-horseshoe ranking cross-check - letter-sound level"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPHS04 is the level companion to [`lrp-rli-hs-003`](../lrp-rli-hs-003/), and the letter-sound counterpart to [`lrp-rli-hs-002`](../lrp-rli-hs-002/) (word-reading level): an **independent Bayesian sensitivity check** on the gradient-boosting predictor ranking for letter-sound **level** (GB `lrp-rli-gbl-009`). A regularized (Finnish) horseshoe sparse regression regresses the concurrent letter-sound count on the standardised **same-wave** levels of the other constructs (plus age), with a subject random intercept for the repeated timepoints; all coefficients share a global-local shrinkage prior. Predictors are ranked by posterior `P(|beta| > delta)` and compared to the GB _cluster_ ranking.

_Ranking cross-check, NOT causal._ A ranking cross-check, not a causal model. In the concurrent-level framing a high naive association is partly same-wave same-construct correlation, so read the ordering as exploratory. Every coefficient is a latent-ability-confounded adjusted association; the randomised causal effect lives in the ITT suite (`lrp-rli-itt-007`).

_Concurrent-level framing._ One row per child × timepoint (`phase_mode="levels"`, ~215 rows over 54 children), mirroring hs-002. Unlike the gain model this omits the RLI-blocks / behaviour covariates: they are wave-sparse (missing at baseline), and requiring them would collapse the levels frame to one row per child, destroying the repeated-measures structure the subject random intercept needs. Weakly-informative priors, not tuned.

## Model

Beta-Binomial concurrent letter-sound count with standardised same-wave predictor levels entering under a regularized-horseshoe prior, plus a subject random intercept $u_{\text{child}}$:

$$
\eta_{i} = \alpha + u_{\text{child}[i]} + \sum_k \beta_k\, z_{k,i},
\qquad
\beta_k \sim \mathcal{N}\!\big(0,\ \tau\, \tilde{\lambda}_k\big).
$$

Construct predictor set: the non-floored concurrent measure levels **other than the outcome** — word reading `W`, receptive/expressive vocabulary `R`/`E`, blending `B`, basic concepts `F`, receptive grammar `T` — plus age.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_horseshoe.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
